common_ai_mistakes.name: unnecessary_list_comprehension
common_ai_mistakes.description: AI wraps generators in list() or uses [x for x in iterable] when the iterable is already a list. Also uses list comprehension just to filter when a generator expression would save memory.
common_ai_mistakes.detection: Detect list(x for x in ...) or [x for x in iterable] patterns.
common_ai_mistakes.fix: Use the iterable directly, or generator expressions for large datasets.

common_ai_mistakes.name: manual_string_building
common_ai_mistakes.description: AI builds strings with + concatenation in loops instead of using join() or f-strings.
common_ai_mistakes.fix: Use ''.join(parts) for building from a list, or f-strings for interpolation.

common_ai_mistakes.name: dict_get_with_none_default
common_ai_mistakes.description: AI uses dict.get(key, None) when None is already the default. Also uses if key in dict: dict[key] instead of dict.get(key).
common_ai_mistakes.fix: Just use dict.get(key) — None is the default. Use dict.get(key, default) only for non-None defaults.

common_ai_mistakes.name: unnecessary_else_after_return
common_ai_mistakes.description: AI puts code in else blocks after if blocks that return. The else is unnecessary — the code after the if already only runs if the condition was false.
common_ai_mistakes.detection: Detect if/return/else patterns where else is unnecessary.
common_ai_mistakes.fix: Remove the else, dedent the code. if condition: return x\nresult = y is cleaner.
